text.title: IH Build-Update Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every IH build-update post must satisfy. Hard rules; designed to prevent "thoughts" posts, vague asks, walls of text, and off-slot timing.

rule.statement: Post body starts with a TL;DR of ≤180 chars on its own line, prefixed "TL;DR:". No preamble before the TL;DR.
rule.rationale: The IH feed truncates around 180 chars; without a TL;DR, the click decision is made on a partial first sentence.

rule.statement: Body includes exactly 3 numeric signals from the last 7 days. Each is a real measurement (MRR, signup count, conv pct, retention pct, churn, refund rate, hours saved). No "we are growing" without a number.
rule.rationale: Posts with ≥3 concrete numbers see 2-3x reply rate vs prose-only posts; below 3 the reader cannot triangulate progress.

rule.statement: Body ends with exactly one ask answerable in <1 min by an outsider. Forbidden asks: "any feedback?", "thoughts?", multi-part questions.
rule.rationale: Specific 1-min asks see 5-10x reply rate vs open-ended asks; multi-part asks fragment replies.

rule.statement: Body length (excluding TL;DR line) is 600-1200 characters. Above 1500 buries the ask; below 400 reads as low-effort.
rule.rationale: The 600-1200 band is the empirical sweet spot for replies-per-impression on IH.

rule.statement: Schedule post for Tuesday 8-10am US Eastern Time. Other slots receive 20-40% less algorithmic distribution per IH reports.
rule.rationale: Tuesday morning ET is the highest-traffic window because founder-mode operators check IH at the start of the workweek.

rule.statement: Publish at most once per 6 days. More frequent posting hurts reach per IH algorithm. Skip weeks where r2 cannot be met rather than padding with weak numbers.
rule.rationale: Posting twice in 72h splits the audience across both posts and burns reputation.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 6-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before publishing.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree run leaf for canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree skip leaf for non-applicable inputs.
